space_description: Investigate a 40% increase in digital ad spend with flat customer acquisition. Explore campaigns, ad spend, clicks, and conversions to find where the budget went.
sample_questions: What is the total ad spend by campaign?
Which campaigns have the lowest conversion rates?
How does CPM trend over the quarter by platform?
What is the click-to-conversion rate by campaign?
Show me spend vs conversions for CAMP-007
Which age groups and geographies are generating the most clicks?

column_configs.description: Marketing channel (Social, Search, Display, Email, Content)
Budget allocated to the campaign in USD
Campaign this spend belongs to — join to campaigns table
Cost per thousand impressions (CPM) in USD
Ad platform (Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, YouTube, etc.)
Age group of the user who clicked (18-24, 25-34, 35-44, 45-54, 55-64, 65+)
Geographic location of the user who clicked
Click that led to this conversion — join to clicks table
Type of conversion (Demo Request, Free Trial, Newsletter, Purchase, Webinar)
Revenue attributed to this conversion in USD (0.0 for non-revenue events)

text_instructions: This space is designed to investigate a 40% quarter-over-quarter increase in digital ad spend with flat net new customer acquisition. Use it to explore campaign performance, audience targeting, and spend efficiency.
Round all currency values to 2 decimal places and all percentages to 2 decimal places. Use USD formatting for monetary amounts.

When users ask about a campaign without specifying which one, ask: 'Which campaign would you like to focus on?' When users ask about performance without specifying a metric, default to click-to-conversion rate.
Glossary: CPM = Cost Per Thousand impressions. CPC = Cost Per Click. CTR = Click-Through Rate. ICP = Ideal Customer Profile. Conversion rate = conversions / clicks * 100. ROAS = Return On Ad Spend.

example_question_sqls.question: What is the total spend and conversion count by campaign?
example_question_sqls.sql:
SELECT
  c.campaign_id,
  c.campaign_name,
  c.channel,
  ROUND(SUM(a.amount_usd), 2) AS total_spend,
  COUNT(DISTINCT cv.conversion_id) AS conversions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT cl.click_id) AS clicks,
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT cv.conversion_id) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT cl.click_id), 0), 2) AS conversion_rate_pct
FROM mkt_budget.`default`.campaigns c
LEFT JOIN mkt_budget.`default`.ad_spend a ON c.campaign_id = a.campaign_id
LEFT JOIN mkt_budget.`default`.clicks cl ON c.campaign_id = cl.campaign_id
LEFT JOIN mkt_budget.`default`.conversions cv ON cl.click_id = cv.click_id
GROUP BY c.campaign_id, c.campaign_name, c.channel
ORDER BY total_spend DESC

example_question_sqls.question: What demographics are clicking on CAMP-007?
example_question_sqls.sql:
SELECT
  cl.user_age_group,
  cl.user_geography,
  COUNT(*) AS click_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT cv.conversion_id) AS conversions,
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT cv.conversion_id) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS conversion_rate_pct
FROM mkt_budget.`default`.clicks cl
LEFT JOIN mkt_budget.`default`.conversions cv ON cl.click_id = cv.click_id
WHERE cl.campaign_id = 'CAMP-007'
GROUP BY cl.user_age_group, cl.user_geography
ORDER BY click_count DESC

example_question_sqls.question: How does CPM trend by month and platform?
example_question_sqls.sql:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('month', a.spend_date) AS month,
  a.platform,
  ROUND(AVG(a.cpm), 2) AS avg_cpm,
  ROUND(SUM(a.amount_usd), 2) AS total_spend
FROM mkt_budget.`default`.ad_spend a
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', a.spend_date), a.platform
ORDER BY month, avg_cpm DESC

example_question_sqls.question: Compare CAMP-007 targeting vs actual click demographics
example_question_sqls.sql:
SELECT
  c.campaign_id,
  c.campaign_name,
  c.target_audience,
  c.target_geography,
  cl.user_age_group,
  cl.user_geography,
  COUNT(*) AS click_count
FROM mkt_budget.`default`.campaigns c
JOIN mkt_budget.`default`.clicks cl ON c.campaign_id = cl.campaign_id
WHERE c.campaign_id = 'CAMP-007'
GROUP BY c.campaign_id, c.campaign_name, c.target_audience, c.target_geography, cl.user_age_group, cl.user_geography
ORDER BY click_count DESC
